sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Scotiabank Lowers EQT (EQT) PT to $63 While Forecasting Persistent Natural Gas Supply Deficits
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price target for EQT Corporation (EQT) to $63 from $67, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, while forecasting persistent natural gas supply deficits in North America. This adjustment is part of a bullish outlook on the natural gas sector, with Scotiabank anticipating that fundamental imbalances will drive commodity prices and energy equities higher. Barclays also reduced its price target for EQT to $64, but kept an Overweight rating, noting the resilience of the upstream sector's cash return model despite macroeconomic volatility.

feed.title: International Paper Company (NYSE:IP) Given Consensus Rating of "Hold" by Analysts
feed.summary: International Paper Company (NYSE:IP) has received a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts, with an average 12-month price target of $49.07. This comes after the company missed Q3 EPS and revenue expectations, reporting negative net margin and return on equity. Insiders and institutions show diverging activity, with a SVP selling shares and several large institutions like BNY Mellon and Norges Bank increasing their holdings.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) Projected to Post Earnings on Thursday

Wall Street maintains a largely positive outlook for the stock, holding an average "Buy" rating and a consensus target price of $193.50. Recent insider selling activity saw approximately 166,068 shares, valued at around $25.5 million, sold in the last quarter, though insiders still own about 1% of the company.

feed.title: BofA Sees Brex Acquisition Complementing and Expanding Capital One's (COF) Spark Card Offerings
feed.summary: Bank of America analyst Mihir Bhatia lowered Capital One's price target to $280 from $294, maintaining a Buy rating, following the company's Q4 2025 earnings and the announcement of its acquisition of Brex. The firm believes the Brex acquisition will complement and expand Capital One's Spark small business card offerings. Despite an increase in net income and revenue, driven largely by the Discover acquisition, BofA revised its 2025/26 EPS forecasts due to anticipated higher operating expenses.

feed.title: Is Analog Devices (ADI) One of Goldman Sachs' Top Semiconductor Stock Picks?
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is highlighted as a top semiconductor stock pick by Goldman Sachs. Both TD Cowen and Stifel recently raised their price targets for ADI, citing strong positioning in industrial markets, resilience, and high-performance analog capabilities. The company maintains a healthy gross profit margin and is expected to deliver strong earnings on February 25th, despite a tempered automotive outlook.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Microsoft (MSFT) Partner to Enhance Early Lung Cancer Detection Through AI
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb and Microsoft have announced a partnership to improve early lung cancer detection using AI. The collaboration will integrate Bristol Myers' oncology expertise with Microsoft's AI-enabled radiology platform, utilizing FDA-cleared AI algorithms to identify lung nodules. This initiative aims to address healthcare disparities by expanding access to early detection in underserved communities, particularly rural hospitals and community clinics across the US.

feed.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Strengthens Position in AI Compute and Infrastructure Ecosystem
feed.summary: Broadcom (AVGO) is considered a top semiconductor stock by Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan, particularly due to its strong position in the AI compute and infrastructure ecosystem. The company is benefiting from increased capital spending on AI infrastructure and networking technology trends. Goldman Sachs' analysis suggests that Broadcom's collaboration with Google on tensor processing units (TPUs) is significantly narrowing the cost efficiency gap with Nvidia's GPU solutions for AI inference.

feed.title: How Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Is Positioning Itself for AI Growth and Server Demand
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is being highlighted by analysts like Goldman Sachs, Bernstein, and KeyBanc Capital Markets for its strong position in AI and server demand. Bernstein raised AMD's price target to $225, with analyst Stacy Rasgon noting its AI advancements and anticipating gains from robust server demand. KeyBanc reaffirmed an Overweight rating with a $270 price target, emphasizing strong momentum in server CPUs and high demand from cloud providers.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Gains Analyst Support as Multi-Year Semiconductor Tailwinds Take Shape
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is receiving strong analyst support, with Goldman Sachs naming it a top semiconductor stock pick. KeyBanc Capital Markets raised its price target to $380, citing AMAT's diversified position and potential gains from increasing deposition and etch intensity in advanced chip manufacturing and conventional DRAM for AI. Barclays also upgraded AMAT to Overweight, projecting a "multi-year earnings tailwind" driven by AI-related spending in the semiconductor industry, noting that concerns about China exposure are already factored into the stock's valuation.

feed.title: The Bull Case For RTX (RTX) Could Change Following Record Backlog And 2026 Growth Guidance - Learn Why
feed.summary: RTX Corporation reported strong 2025 results with US$88,603 million in sales and US$6,732 million in net income, and provided optimistic 2026 guidance including 5% to 6% organic sales growth. The company achieved a record US$268 billion backlog and secured significant defense contracts, underscoring strong near-term revenue visibility. While the stock has risen and is potentially undervalued, investors are cautioned about increased expectations and the potential impact of execution missteps.

feed.title: Summit Insights upgrades Lam Research stock rating to Buy from Hold
feed.summary: Summit Insights upgraded Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) from Hold to Buy, citing the semiconductor equipment maker's potential to capture a disproportionate share in upcoming technology transitions across memory segments. The firm believes Lam Research's risk-reward profile is favorable despite recent stock price increases, backed by strong execution and industry conditions. This upgrade follows Lam Research's strong Q2 FY2026 earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue.

feed.title: Post (POST) Expected to Announce Earnings on Thursday

The company exceeded expectations in the previous quarter, reporting $2.09 EPS on strong revenue growth. While analyst consensus projects annual EPS of $6 for the current fiscal year and $7 for the next, the stock faces challenges including weak technicals, a low quick ratio, and high debt-to-equity.

feed.title: Boston Scientific Stock: Analyst Estimates & Ratings

Analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating for the company, with a mean price target representing a significant premium to its current price levels. The company's recent earnings surprise history and demand for its heart devices also contribute to investor optimism.

feed.title: SMCI $31.21 pre-market: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ) Feb 3 earnings focus

feed.title: Does Prologis' AI Data Center Ambition And 2026 EPS Outlook Change The Bull Case For PLD?
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc. recently reported its 2025 full-year results, including sales of US$8,158.90 million and net income of US$3,328.23 million, while providing 2026 EPS guidance of US$3.70 to US$4.00. The company highlighted a new ambition to support AI data centers within its existing logistics facilities, citing potential capacity for 10 gigawatts of power. This strategic shift introduces a new dimension to Prologis' investment narrative, emphasizing a potentially meaningful catalyst despite existing concerns about balance sheet scrutiny and execution risks.

feed.title: Southern Copper (NYSE:SCCO) Shares Set to Split on Tuesday, February 10th
feed.summary: Southern Copper (NYSE:SCCO) announced a 1.0085-for-1 stock split to become effective on February 10th, with new shares distributed after market close on February 9th. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $1.00 per share, implying a 2.0% yield and a 75.47% payout ratio. Despite recent strong earnings beats and favorable copper demand, concerns linger over management's guidance of lower output in 2027–28 and mixed analyst sentiment.

feed.title: TJX Stock Falls -6.1% With A 7-day Losing Spree On Retail Sector Fears
feed.summary: TJX stock experienced a significant 6.1% drop, extending its losing streak to seven consecutive days. This decline is attributed to growing fears surrounding the retail sector, impacting investor confidence in the company.

feed.summary: The article title asks whether a 21.0% fall in Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) stock presents a buying opportunity. feed.title: Currency Hedged ETFs Could Be Interesting U.S. Election Plays
feed.summary: Ahead of the first presidential debate, investors are considering currency-hedged ETFs like HEDJ and DXJ as potential plays on U.S. election volatility. Goldman Sachs suggests the U.S. dollar could strengthen, particularly under certain election outcomes, benefiting these funds. The debate's early timing could lead to market reactions sooner than usual, making hedges a timely consideration.

feed.title: Does ONB's Strong Q4 and Bremer Integration Redefine Old National Bancorp's Investment Narrative?
feed.summary: Old National Bancorp reported strong Q4 2025 results with higher net interest income and net income, alongside increased net charge-offs. The bank also completed a significant subordinated note offering and a share repurchase program, while highlighting the successful integration of Bremer Bank. This performance reinforces the bank's strategy of focusing on scale, core deposit strength, and fee income, though rising net charge-offs and new debt warrant investor awareness.

feed.title: A Look At General Electric’s Valuation After Strong 2025 Results And Growing Aerospace And Vernova Order Momentum
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) reported strong 2025 full-year results with US$45.9b revenue and US$8.7b net income, yet its shares saw a short-term decline. Despite this pullback, the stock is considered 13.9% undervalued with a fair value of $339.69, driven by significant productivity gains and increased free cash flow. The article suggests that while longer-term momentum remains strong, with a 1-year total shareholder return of 47.74%, investors should consider the P/E ratio and other risks before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Is There Now An Opportunity In Pfizer (PFE) After Recent Pandemic Demand Reset?
feed.summary: Pfizer's stock appears undervalued according to Simply Wall St's analysis, with a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggesting a 56.8% discount from its intrinsic value and its P/E ratio being lower than both the industry and its estimated Fair Ratio. The company's recent repositioning post-pandemic demand and its product pipeline are key factors influencing investor perception, making it a potential opportunity for long-term investors. Simply Wall St encourages investors to use its "Narratives" tool to customize their valuation based on their own outlook for the company's future financials.

feed.title: HIVE Digital Technologies (TSXV:HIVE) Is Down 9.7% After Analyst Downgrade And Governance Proposals - Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies (TSXV:HIVE) has seen its stock price decline by nearly 10% following an analyst downgrade and the release of materials for its upcoming annual meeting. The downgrade questions the returns on HIVE's Bitcoin mining build-out and its AI cloud services positioning, despite strong Bitcoin production and planned capacity expansion. Investors are now weighing these concerns against the company's growth plans and existing risks like capital efficiency and equity issuance.

feed.title: Ford Energy Launch Adds New Dimension To Ford Motor Valuation Story
feed.summary: Ford Motor has launched a new battery energy storage business, Ford Energy, appointing Lisa Drake as its president. The company will repurpose its BlueOval SK plant in Kentucky to manufacture battery storage systems, signaling Ford's expansion into broader energy solutions beyond electric vehicles. This strategic move introduces a new segment for investors to monitor, providing an additional dimension to Ford's valuation within the electrification and energy infrastructure markets.
